notes: Body height, standard OMOP concept, confirmed. Range in cm.
Body weight, standard OMOP concept, confirmed. Range in kg.
Body mass index (BMI) [Ratio], LOINC 39156-5, confirmed. Range in kg/m^2.
Systolic blood pressure, confirmed. Range in mmHg.
Diastolic blood pressure, confirmed. Range in mmHg.
Glucose [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma, LOINC 2345-7, confirmed. Range in mg/dL.
Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood, LOINC 4548-4, confirmed. Range in % (NGSP).
LOINC 2085-9 (Cholesterol in HDL) -- real LOINC code, exact OMOP concept_id not verified, check AoU Data Browser before use. Range in mg/dL.
LOINC 13457-7 (Cholesterol in LDL, calculated) -- real LOINC code, exact OMOP concept_id not verified, check AoU Data Browser before use. Range in mg/dL.
LOINC 2571-8 (Triglyceride) -- real LOINC code, exact OMOP concept_id not verified, check AoU Data Browser before use. Range in mg/dL.

LOINC 8280-0 (Waist Circumference at umbilicus by Tape measure) -- real LOINC code, exact OMOP concept_id not verified, check AoU Data Browser before use. Range in cm. Physical Measurements module, collected alongside height/weight -- expect similar completeness. Pairs with hip_circumference below for waist-hip ratio, a body-fat-distribution trait with genetic architecture partly distinct from BMI.

LOINC 8286-7 (Hip circumference by Tape measure) -- real LOINC code, exact OMOP concept_id not verified, check AoU Data Browser before use. Range in cm. Physical Measurements module; see waist_circumference above.

LOINC 8867-4 (Heart rate) -- real LOINC code, exact OMOP concept_id not verified, check AoU Data Browser before use. Range in bpm. In-clinic single-reading pulse, Physical Measurements module -- distinct from fitbit_resting_heart_rate below (continuous at-home signal, noisier per-reading but far more repeated measures per person).

LOINC 2093-3 (Cholesterol in Serum or Plasma) -- real LOINC code, exact OMOP concept_id not verified, check AoU Data Browser before use. Range in mg/dL. Standard lipid panel component, typically ordered alongside HDL/LDL/triglycerides -- completeness should track those closely.

LOINC 2160-0 (Creatinine in Serum or Plasma) -- real LOINC code, exact OMOP concept_id not verified, check AoU Data Browser before use. Range in mg/dL. Part of virtually every basic metabolic panel -- one of the most complete labs in EHR data generally. Kidney-function trait; could later derive eGFR (needs age/sex/race-based equation) as a follow-on phenotype.

LOINC 718-7 (Hemoglobin in Blood) -- real LOINC code, exact OMOP concept_id not verified, check AoU Data Browser before use. Range in g/dL. CBC component, extremely commonly ordered. Sex-stratified reference ranges are wide -- this pipeline's within-sex standardization step is a particularly good fit for it.

LOINC 6690-2 (Leukocytes in Blood) -- real LOINC code, exact OMOP concept_id not verified, check AoU Data Browser before use. Range in x10^3/uL. CBC component. Well-studied immune trait with many known GWAS loci; right-skewed, good invnorm-transform candidate.

LOINC 777-3 (Platelets in Blood) -- real LOINC code, exact OMOP concept_id not verified, check AoU Data Browser before use. Range in x10^3/uL. CBC component. One of the more heritable common blood-count traits (e.g. ARHGEF3 locus); a good positive control for pipeline validation since the signal is well-established in the literature.

LOINC 1742-6 (Alanine aminotransferase) -- real LOINC code, exact OMOP concept_id not verified, check AoU Data Browser before use. Range in U/L. Liver enzyme, common in metabolic/liver panels but ordered somewhat less universally than CBC/BMP -- expect more missingness than the CBC/BMP/lipid rows above. Strongly right-skewed.

Not a single measurement -- waist_circumference / hip_circumference for the same person, ideally same visit. concept_id holds both underlying concept_ids (waist,hip, in that order), matching waist_circumference/hip_circumference above once those are confirmed. source == 'derived_ratio' is NOT wired up in pull_phenotype() -- needs a new code path that pulls both concept_ids, joins on person_id (and nearest measurement_date, not just 'most recent value' independently for each, or the pairing could mix measurements from different visits), and computes the ratio; a materially different shape than every other row here. Body-fat-distribution trait, genetically partly distinct from BMI/overall adiposity -- WHR adjusted for BMI (WHRadjBMI) is the more standard GWAS phenotype in the literature (GIANT consortium) and shows notably stronger genetic signal in women than men; adjusting for BMI isn't implemented here (would mean using bmi as a regression covariate for another phenotype, not currently how pull_covariates()/build_covariate_sets() work) but is worth keeping in mind if this trait's results look unexpectedly weak or sex-inconsistent.
